authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency banking guidance for third-party lifecycle management, governance, due diligence, monitoring, and issue escalation in bank-fintech arrangements.

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint banking-agency guide that operationalizes due-diligence domains and record expectations specific to fintech partnerships.

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Management Review Examination Procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB examination procedures for compliance management systems, complaint handling, monitoring, corrective action, and board-management oversight.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers (Regulation E)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary consumer-payments source for disclosures, error resolution, prepaid account handling, and dispute timing obligations relevant to fintech payment products.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Examination Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interagency workflow reference for CIP, CDD, monitoring, suspicious-activity escalation, and examiner evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: KYC, monitoring, and escalation boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Customer Due Diligence Requirements for Financial Institutions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary FinCEN rule source for beneficial-ownership and customer-due-diligence requirements that determine minimum KYC evidence expectations.
used_for: cannot-decide boundary when records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury - Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary sanctions-compliance framework for management commitment, risk assessment, internal controls, testing, and remediation evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sanctions control evidence and remediation review

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS v4.0.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card control standard establishing evidence, scoping, testing, and remediation expectations for card-enabled fintech products.

workflow_stages.name: Program and product scoping
workflow_stages.description: Identify the product type, partner-bank or licensed-entity model, jurisdictions, and governing source set before any compliance claim is made.

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Translate consumer-protection, KYC, sanctions, third-party oversight, and payment-data expectations into a control matrix tied to owners and systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence readiness and review cadence
workflow_stages.description: Confirm complaints, disputes, KYC, screening, vendor oversight, and payment-data evidence exists, is current, and is retrievable from authoritative systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and remediation management
workflow_stages.description: Package control failures, policy-practice conflicts, and missing artifacts into owner-tagged remediation notes with review cadence and escalation triggers.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate legal interpretation, AML adjudication, sanctions decisions, fraud decisions, regulator responses, and formal certification outside the lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Complaint logs and complaint taxonomy evidence
Dispute, chargeback, and error-resolution case records
OFAC screening logs and sanctions issue records
PCI evidence, attestations, and scoping records
Issue logs, remediation trackers, and review packets
systems_of_record: Security compliance repositories for PCI evidence

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing fintech-specific prerequisites.
Map obligations to controls, evidence owners, review cadence, and escalation owners.
Prepare evidence-readiness summaries, control matrices, review packets, and remediation notes.
Escalate legal, regulator-facing, AML, sanctions, fraud, or certification questions to the proper owner.
escalate: Required product, jurisdiction, partner, or system context is missing.
Public sources and tenant policy materially conflict on consumer protection, KYC, sanctions, licensing, or partner oversight posture.
A control failure affects customer harm risk, suspicious-activity handling, sanctions posture, or regulator commitments.
A partner bank, auditor, or regulator response requires formal owner review.
refuse: Any request for legal interpretation, licensing determination, or formal signoff.
Requests to clear sanctions alerts, decide SAR filing, or adjudicate fraud cases.
Requests to certify PCI or overall regulatory compliance inside this lane.
Requests that use fintech as a label while omitting the actual product, record, and authority context.

domain_failure_modes: Treating sponsor-bank or embedded-finance oversight as generic GRC work and omitting product, jurisdiction, or partner-bank dependencies.
Marking controls as satisfied when complaint, dispute, KYC, sanctions, or third-party evidence is missing from systems of record.
Resolving conflicting policy or regulatory interpretations without escalating to legal or program owners.
Blurring compliance support with AML adjudication, sanctions decisions, fraud investigation, or regulator-facing authority.
Claiming PCI, regulatory, or partner-bank review readiness without named evidence owners, review cadence, and open-gap disclosure.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State licensing posture varies by product and entity structure
unresolved_ambiguities.note: Whether a tenant acts as program manager, processor, lender, broker, or licensed money transmitter materially changes the source stack. The pack therefore requires tenant runtime facts and escalates legal interpretation rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Direct versus sponsor-bank ownership of specific controls
unresolved_ambiguities.note: Fintech programs differ on whether KYC, sanctions, complaint handling, dispute operations, and card-data controls are owned by the bank, fintech, or vendor. The pack treats this as a required owner-mapping fact rather than a default assumption.
